en_title: 25 Years Later, Atlantis: The Lost Empire Has Become One of Disney’s Most Important Movies
cn_title: 25年后，《亚特兰蒂斯》成迪士尼关键之作
subtitle: 看点来了
opus_summary: 2001年上映的《亚特兰蒂斯：失落的帝国》在票房和口碑上遭遇双重失败，却凭借家庭录像逐渐积累起忠实粉丝，并在25年后被重新评价为迪士尼最具影响力的动画之一。

paragraphs.en: If the average person was asked to name some of Disney’s most influential animated classics, you’d probably hear names like Snow White, The Little Mermaid, Beauty and the Beast, or The Lion King. Far less likely would be Atlantis: The Lost Empire , which was released 25 years ago this month on June 15, 2001. Coming off what is widely considered one of the studio’s strongest eras with the Disney Renaissance of the 1990s, Atlantis was an attempt by the team behind The Hunchback of Notre Dame — directors Gary Trousdale and Kirk Wise, producer Don Hahn, and screenwriter Tab Murphy — to chart a new course for Disney theatrical animation, one styled after Adventureland at Disney’s theme parks.
paragraphs.cn: 如果问普通人迪士尼最具影响力的经典动画有哪些，你大概会听到《白雪公主》《小美人鱼》《美女与野兽》或《狮子王》这些名字。而《亚特兰蒂斯：失落的帝国》则很少被提及——该片于25年前的这个月，即2001年6月15日上映。彼时，迪士尼刚刚走出被普遍视为工作室最辉煌时期之一的1990年代「迪士尼复兴」时期，《亚特兰蒂斯》正是《钟楼怪人》主创团队——导演Gary Trousdale和Kirk Wise、制片人Don Hahn、编剧Tab Murphy——试图为迪士尼院线动画开辟一条新路的尝试，这种风格参照了迪士尼主题乐园中的「探险世界」。

paragraphs.en: Sadly, that attempt didn’t fare so well. Atlantis flopped at the box office, and was met with mixed to negative reviews. You might think that would be the end of the story, but sometimes, movies have longer shelf lives than their initial reception would suggest. Atlantis developed a strong cult following after making its way to home video, and has been far better received now than it was on its original release. But more than that, the film has arguably helped shape the entire direction of North American theatrical animation by essentially being the road not taken for animated features . How did this happen? Let’s take a look...
paragraphs.cn: 遗憾的是，这一尝试并不顺利。《亚特兰蒂斯》票房惨败，并收获了褒贬不一乃至负面为主的评价。你或许以为故事到此为止，但有些电影的寿命比最初的反响更为长久。在发行家庭录像后，《亚特兰蒂斯》逐渐培养起了一批忠实拥趸，如今它的口碑远胜于当初上映时。然而更重要的是，这部电影可以说帮助塑造了北美院线动画的整个发展方向，因为它本质上就是动画长片一条未曾走过的路。这是怎么回事？让我们来看看……

paragraphs.en: When it comes to the eras of Disney animation history, the period after the much-lauded Disney Renaissance — generally considered to have ended with Tarzan in 1999 — has rarely been placed in the same conversation. Yes, there were some not-great movies released during that period, but Atlantis has always gotten the short end of the stick given the pedigree of talent involved. Gary Trousdale and Kirk Wise directed Beauty and the Beast and The Hunchback of Notre Dame, two of the most artistically resonant animated movies of the 1990s, and Tab Murphy had penned the screenplays for both Hunchback and Tarzan. The film has a strong cast, sharply directed action sequences, a beautiful musical score by James Newton Howard, and a gorgeous art style based on the work of famed Hellboy comic artist Mike Mignola. This was not some low-rent production lacking in major talent, and the strength of those artists and their vision is why the film maintains a fanbase to this day.
paragraphs.cn: 谈到迪士尼动画史的各个时期，备受赞誉的「迪士尼复兴」之后——一般认为以1999年的《泰山》为终点——这段时期很少被相提并论。诚然，那期间上映了一些不太出色的电影，但考虑到《亚特兰蒂斯》创作团队的实力，它始终是最被埋没的。Gary Trousdale和Kirk Wise曾执导过《美女与野兽》与《钟楼怪人》——这两部堪称1990年代艺术感染力最强的动画电影之一；而Tab Murphy则同时为《钟楼怪人》和《泰山》撰写了剧本。这部电影拥有阵容强大的配音演员、调度利落的动作场面、James Newton Howard创作的优美配乐，以及以著名《地狱男爵》漫画艺术家Mike Mignola的作品为蓝本的华丽画风。这并非一部缺乏顶尖人才的低成本制作，而正是这些艺术家及其愿景的力量让本片至今仍保有粉丝基础。

paragraphs.en: I spoke to Trousdale and Murphy about the film, and asked them what they think has kept Atlantis alive in the popular imagination.
paragraphs.cn: 我采访了Trousdale和Murphy，询问他们认为是什么让《亚特兰蒂斯》在大众记忆中经久不衰。

paragraphs.en: “I like to think that it's a decent film,” said Trousdale. “That it's entertaining and fun, and the characters are fun. I mean, we kind of patterned it after the live-action Disney films from the late '50s and early '60s, the Jules Verne 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea, that kind of action adventure film… it didn't do as well with the adults who were expecting talking lions and singing mice and all that. And there was gunfire and there were no songs. We found out that the kids really enjoyed it, but they didn't really have a voice."
paragraphs.cn: Trousdale说道：「我愿意相信这是一部体面的电影，既有趣又好玩，角色也讨人喜欢。其实我们多少是照着50年代末60年代初的迪士尼真人电影来拍的，比如儒勒·凡尔纳的《海底两万里》那种动作冒险片……但那些期待会说话的狮子和唱歌的老鼠的成年人就不太买账了。而且片中有枪战，也没有歌曲。我们发现孩子们其实非常喜欢，但他们没什么话语权。」

paragraphs.en: Murphy agreed, saying that “a lot of kids watched it on home video and wore out VHS tapes. So there was this growing fanbase [that] I think myself and a lot of the filmmakers were unaware of, because there's no way to track home video, how the fanbase grows out of sharing a VHS or ultimately the DVD and all that stuff.”
paragraphs.cn: Murphy表示认同，他说：「很多孩子在家里看录像带，把VHS都看坏了。于是逐渐形成了一个不断扩大的粉丝群体，我想我和很多创作者当时都没意识到，因为家庭录像的传播无从追踪，粉丝群体通过分享VHS、乃至后来的DVD等渠道发展起来，这些都无法统计。」

paragraphs.en: The sense of adventure and exploration with a vivid group of characters that the film manages to convey in a tight 90-minute runtime is one of its best aspects. Trousdale elaborated on how the creative team convinced the studio to move away from the Broadway musicals that had defined the last decade of Disney films.
paragraphs.cn: 电影在短短90分钟内呈现出的冒险感和探索精神，加上一群生动鲜明的角色，是它最大的亮点之一。Trousdale详细讲述了创作团队是如何说服迪士尼工作室，放弃过去十年定义迪士尼电影风格的百老汇音乐剧模式。

paragraphs.en: “I think it might've been Kirk or maybe it was Don that came up with the notion that when you go to Disneyland and you're headed straight for Cinderella's Castle, you go through it and you're in Fantasyland. And that's the movies that we're doing for the last 70 years. But if you hang a left, you're in Adventureland. It's still a Disney land. And that's what got the executives to go, ‘Oh yeah, you're right.’”
paragraphs.cn: 「我记得可能是Kirk，也或许是Don，提出了这样一个想法：你去迪士尼乐园，直奔灰姑娘城堡，穿过去就是幻想世界。这也是我们过去70年来一直在拍的电影。但如果你左转，就来到了探险世界，同样是迪士尼的一片天地。正是这个比喻让高管们恍然大悟：‘哦对，你说的没错。’」

paragraphs.en: Add in evocative location, vehicle, and creature design — the Leviathan is still one of the best monsters to ever appear in a Disney film — and you have a movie that has continued to charm both older audiences revisiting it and newcomers who discover it. But the timing of its release caused a lot of problems; Atlantis was a 2D animated adventure at a time when 3D animated comedies were becoming the new model for theatrical animation... and that’s where everything started to fall apart.
paragraphs.cn: 再加上令人回味的场景、载具和生物设计——利维坦至今仍是迪士尼电影中设计最出色的怪物之一——这部电影既能持续吸引重温它的老观众，也能让新观众一见倾心。但它的上映时机引发了诸多问题；《亚特兰蒂斯》是一部2D动画冒险片，而当时3D动画喜剧正逐渐成为影院动画的新模式……一切也正是从那时开始溃散的。

paragraphs.en: Atlantis is a flashpoint in film history because it sits right on the dividing line between 20th century and 21st century theatrical animation. It’s a 2D animated adventure film with some 3D elements — a blend of two styles that would largely become segregated in the years to follow. It’s not quite akin to the films of the Disney Renaissance, yet it’s also not quite what feature animation would become after the turn of the millennium with the rise of Pixar and Dreamworks’ 3D movies that would change the industry forever. Atlantis is fascinating because it was part of a small wave of 2D/3D hybrid adventure features — Atlantis in 2001, Treasure Planet in 2002, and Sinbad: Legend of the Seven Seas in 2003. These movies, all of which bombed at the box office, ran in parallel to the mode of storytelling popularized by the likes of Toy Story and Shrek.
paragraphs.cn: 《亚特兰蒂斯》是电影史上的一个转折点，因为它正好处于20世纪与21世纪影院动画的分界线上。它是一部带有部分3D元素的2D动画冒险片——两种风格的融合，但在随后的岁月里却基本走向了分道扬镳。它既不完全类似迪士尼复兴时期的电影，也不完全契合千禧年之后，随着皮克斯和梦工厂的3D电影崛起并永远改变行业格局后，长片动画的形态。《亚特兰蒂斯》之所以迷人，是因为它属于一小波2D/3D混合冒险电影之一——2001年的《亚特兰蒂斯》，2002年的《星银岛》，以及2003年的《辛巴达七海传奇》。这些影片票房全部惨败，与《玩具总动员》和《怪物史莱克》所引领的叙事模式并驾而行。

paragraphs.en: When asked about the impact of 3D movies on Atlantis, Trousdale said “I kind of pin it on the first Shrek for putting the nail in the coffin of 2D animation. And there's still a big appetite for it, but not as much in this country as say Japan or Italy. It's almost like a boutique kind of medium here to see 2D animation because 3D animation has taken over.”
paragraphs.cn: 当被问及3D电影对《亚特兰蒂斯》的影响时，Trousdale表示：「我几乎把2D动画的棺材板钉死在了第一部《怪物史莱克》上。观众对2D动画仍有很大需求，但在这个国家不如日本或意大利那么强烈。在这里，看2D动画几乎成了一种精品小众媒介，因为3D动画已经占据了统治地位。」

paragraphs.en: Murphy echoed the sentiment, saying “there was a new kid on the block, a new toy glistening in the movie theaters, and that was CGI, that was Shrek. And so Atlantis got released at a time when all eyes were sort of moving off of Disney a bit onto DreamWorks.”
paragraphs.cn: Murphy也表达了类似的看法，他说：「电影院里来了一个新秀，一个闪闪发光的新玩具，那就是CGI，那就是《怪物史莱克》。所以，《亚特兰蒂斯》上映的时机，正好是所有人的目光正逐渐从迪士尼转向梦工厂的时候。」

paragraphs.en: Not only was Shrek a massive success that spawned a long-running franchise, it opened the door for Dreamworks Animation to become a major player in the theatrical space with projects like Madagascar, Kung Fu Panda, and How to Train Your Dragon. While Disney struggled until around 2010 to find a new direction for its main animated division, Pixar was in its golden age in the early 2000s with movies like Monsters Inc, Finding Nemo, and The Incredibles.
paragraphs.cn: 《怪物史莱克》不仅大获成功并打造了一个长寿系列，还为梦工厂动画打开了通向影院市场的大门，随后才有《马达加斯加》《功夫熊猫》《驯龙高手》等作品。迪士尼的动画部门在2010年前后才艰难找到新方向，而皮克斯在2000年代早期正值黄金时期，推出了《怪兽电力公司》《海底总动员》《超人总动员》等电影。

paragraphs.en: That Atlantis stood in such contrast to emerging industry trends — that it was staunchly not part of the "new way" of doing things — is both what has made it so distinct in retrospect and so out of step with what audiences were coming to crave in 2001. Atlantis was simultaneously behind the curve and ahead of its time, featuring old-fashioned pulp serial sensibility in a market that longed for the novelty of fully computer-generated features and an easygoing comedic flavor. Yet those same creative instincts that condemned Atlantis’ theatrical prospects are exactly what has kept it alive as a cultural artifact a quarter century later. In the same way that Event Horizon doomed space horror movies for the foreseeable future only to become a touchstone for the format because there were so few other examples, Atlantis is one of the only entries in a subgenre that never really was: the blockbuster animated action adventure.
paragraphs.cn: 正因《亚特兰蒂斯》与新兴行业趋势如此格格不入——它坚定地不属于「新做法」——才让它在回顾时显得如此独特，也让它与2001年观众的口味脱节。《亚特兰蒂斯》既落后于时代又超前于时代，它带着老派廉价通俗连载小说的感觉，而当年市场渴望的是全电脑动画的新奇和轻松的喜剧风味。然而，正是这些让《亚特兰蒂斯》在影院前景黯淡的创作本能，在四分之一个世纪后让它作为文化遗存继续生存。就像《黑洞表面》曾让太空恐怖片在相当长一段时间内无人问津，后来却因同类作品太少而成为标杆一样，《亚特兰蒂斯》也是一个几乎从未真正存在的亚类型——大片级动画动作冒险片——中仅有的几部作品之一。

paragraphs.en: Atlantis’s financial failure, alongside its siblings Treasure Planet and Sinbad, didn't just cause Disney to abandon the Adventureland experiment, but also heralded the almost complete disappearance of 2D animation from the theatrical space by the 2010s.
paragraphs.cn: 《亚特兰蒂斯》的票房失利，连同它的姊妹篇《星银岛》和《辛巴达》，不仅让迪士尼放弃了冒险乐园实验，也预示着2D动画在2010年代几乎从影院空间消失。

paragraphs.en: The speed at which 2D animation fell out of favor in North America post-Atlantis is startling when you actually look at the timeline. Even with Pixar’s release schedule being nearly annual, Disney’s main theatrical division started making the transition to 3D in earnest as early as 2005, putting out Chicken Little, Meet the Robinsons, and Bolt in quick succession (they’d done a one-off 3D experiment in 2000 with Dinosaur). Disney’s last 2D animated film was 2011's Winnie the Pooh, with every single theatrical movie since then being 3D. That’s not to say that Atlantis was the only reason for the industry's reinvention, but the movie not doing well theatrically did result in a massive shift in Disney’s operational strategy since the film was being groomed to be the next big franchise in the studio’s catalogue.
paragraphs.cn: 当你审视时间线时，2D动画在《亚特兰蒂斯》之后在北美失宠之迅速令人震惊。即便皮克斯的发行节奏接近每年一部，迪士尼的动画电影部门也早在2005年就开始认真转向3D，接连推出了《四眼天鸡》《拜见罗宾逊一家》和《闪电狗》（他们在2000年已用《恐龙》做过一次性的3D实验）。迪士尼最后一部2D动画电影是2011年的《小熊维尼》，此后每一部影院动画都是3D。这并不是说《亚特兰蒂斯》是行业重塑的唯一原因，但该片票房不佳确实导致迪士尼运营策略发生巨变，因为这部电影原本被培养成制片厂片单中的下一个重磅系列。

paragraphs.en: Atlantis’ creative team did have a concept for a theatrical sequel which was separate from the direct-to-video sequel that was actually produced. Trousdale confirmed that Helga Sinclair would have returned as the villain.
paragraphs.cn: 《亚特兰蒂斯》的创意团队确实为一部院线续集构思了概念，与后来实际制作的音像版续集不同。Trousdale证实，反派Helga Sinclair将在续集中回归。

paragraphs.en: "There were maybe a couple outline pages written. I don't think there was ever a script, but yeah, it was an idea that Milo and Kida are exploring the other cavern networks and seeing who else is down there. And they run into Helga and she's a video game boss now. She's a steampunk cyborg who's crystal powered and she has this band of pirates and rogues.” Trousdale also discussed Disney’s plans for Atlantis theme park attractions, saying that “they were going to build an Atlantis ride in Florida. They were going to retrofit the submarine ride in Anaheim, California… they were going to put the Leviathan down there and change the architecture. But yeah, all that fell through.”
paragraphs.cn: 「可能只写了几页大纲。我不记得有完整的剧本，但没错，想法是迈罗和姬塔在探索其他洞穴网络，看看下面还有谁。然后他们遇到了Helga，她就成了个电子游戏Boss。她是个蒸汽朋克赛博格，由水晶驱动，率领着一帮海盗和流氓。」Trousdale还谈到了迪士尼为《亚特兰蒂斯》建设主题公园景点的计划，说「他们原本要在佛罗里达建一个亚特兰蒂斯游乐项目。他们打算改造加州阿纳海姆的潜水艇项目……想把利维坦放下去，改变建筑风格。但最后这一切都泡汤了。」

paragraphs.en: Murphy had his own idea for a potential follow-up to Atlantis — a prequel.
paragraphs.cn: Murphy对《亚特兰蒂斯》的潜在续集也有自己的想法——一部前传。

paragraphs.en: "My preference would've been to actually tell the story of Princess Kida from princess to warrior, and the movie would end when Milo and the team arrived… I thought there would be a great story in there, her story.”
paragraphs.cn: 「我其实更想讲述姬塔公主从公主到战士的故事，电影会在迈罗和团队到达时结束……我觉得那里头有个很棒的故事，她的故事。」

paragraphs.en: Instead, none of these things — including the planned Team Atlantis television series — would come to pass. Atlantis has since largely been ignored by Disney, to much frustration from those who love the film.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，这些计划——包括原定的Team Atlantis电视动画——无一实现。此后，《亚特兰蒂斯》基本被迪士尼忽视，这让喜爱本片的影迷大为沮丧。

paragraphs.en: “One of my current gripes," said Trousdale, "is that Disney has kind of elevated all the Disney princesses to this new level, and there's all the princess girls, and Kida isn't one of them. They don't count her. It's like, ‘come on guys, she's literally a princess. She's a queen for Christ's sake.’"
paragraphs.cn: Trousdale说：「我目前的一个不满是，迪士尼把所有迪士尼公主都拔到了一个新高度，搞了一堆公主女孩，姬塔却不在其中。他们不算她。真是的，拜托，她可是货真价实的公主，看在老天的份上，她都当上女王了。」

paragraphs.en: Murphy also spoke about the impact Princess Kida had on his interactions with fans: “I can't tell you how many women and how many Black women have come up to me and said, ‘That movie made such an impression on me, for that character alone, because it was the first time I sat in a theater and I looked up as a little girl and I saw somebody that looked like me as a character in a Disney movie.’”
paragraphs.cn: Murphy还谈到姬塔公主对他与粉丝交流的影响：「我都数不清有多少女性、多少黑人女性走到我面前说，『那部电影给我留下了极深刻的印象，单凭那个角色，因为那是我第一次坐在影院里，作为一个小女孩抬起头，看到迪士尼电影里有一个角色长得像我。』」

paragraphs.en: Disney doesn’t acknowledge Kida as an official Disney Princess despite the fact that she was a Black princess who preceded Tiana from The Princess and the Frog by nearly a decade, but as Trousdale said: “We didn’t make enough money. That was the bottom line.”
paragraphs.cn: 迪士尼不承认姬塔是官方迪士尼公主，尽管她是一位比《公主与青蛙》中的蒂安娜早近十年的黑人公主，但如Trousdale所说：「我们没赚够钱。这才是根本原因。」

paragraphs.en: Regarding the ramifications of Disney ultimately moving away from 2D, Trousdale said that “we've lost a lot of the expertise, a lot of the crews, the people that did the animation, the actual animators, the cleanup artists, the cel painters. Well, they didn't paint cels anymore. It was on a digital screen… most of those people have retired or died off or just moved on to other things. And every so often you'll hear of a studio or you'll hear of Disney going, ‘We're going to bring back 2D animation.’ And my reaction is usually like, ‘Well, good luck. You got to find the people to do it now. You had the people and you let them all go and they're gone now.’” As for how Disney as a company would be different if Atlantis was successful, Trousdale said “they might still have a 2D division because it had been theoretically proven that the 2D was still viable and was still marketable, so they probably wouldn't have ditched all of the 2D. That genre of picture, the action adventure, the non-musical, might have a little bit bigger space in the building.”
paragraphs.cn: 谈到迪士尼最终放弃二维动画的后果时，Trousdale表示：「我们失去了大量专业人才，失去了许多团队，失去了做动画的人——真正的动画师、修线师、赛璐珞上色师。嗯，他们早就不在赛璐珞片上作画了，是在数字屏幕上……这些人大多数已经退休、去世，或者转行去干别的了。偶尔会听说有工作室，或者听说迪士尼自己放出话来，说要‘重新拾起二维动画’。我的反应通常是：‘好吧，祝你好运。你现在得先找到能做的人。当初你们手里有人，却让所有人走了，现在他们都离开了。’」至于如果当年《亚特兰蒂斯》大获成功，迪士尼这家公司会有什么不同，Trousdale说道：「他们可能还会保留一个二维动画部门，因为理论上已经证明二维动画仍然可行、仍然有市场，所以他们大概不会把二维全部砍掉。那种类型片——动作冒险、非音乐剧——在公司里也许会拥有更大一点的存在空间。」

paragraphs.en: Atlantis: The Lost Empire was a crucial junction for Disney. It represented a moment in time where the company was open to moving in a new creative direction after years of success. That direction was cut short by Atlantis failing at the box office, and the consequences — not just for Disney, but the entire animated film industry — are still being felt to this day. But while Atlantis may not have found its audience on release, it has in the years that followed. It may not be the success it needed, but it has at least received the love it deserves.
paragraphs.cn: 《亚特兰蒂斯：失落的帝国》对迪士尼而言是一个关键节点。它代表了一个时刻——公司在多年成功后愿意转向新的创作方向。但《亚特兰蒂斯》票房失利，让这个方向戛然而止，其后果——不仅对迪士尼，对整个动画电影行业——至今仍在持续发酵。然而，虽然影片上映时没有找到它的观众，却在随后的岁月里收获了影迷。它或许没能取得所需的商业成功，但至少得到了应得的爱。

paragraphs.en: Carlos Morales writes novels, articles, and Mass Effect essays. You can follow his fixations on Twitter .
paragraphs.cn: Carlos Morales撰写小说、文章和《质量效应》评论。你可以在X上关注他的种种执念。
